title: A fiber mat, method of making the fiber mat, and bituminous roofing product
abstract: A fiber mat includes a unitary assembly of fibers including at least a first set of fibers and at least a first binder comprising an organic resin, wherein the unitary assembly of fibers includes a minority portion and a majority portion different than the minority portion, wherein the fiber mat provides at least a 5% increase in tear when placed in a bituminous roofing product compared to an equivalent bituminous roofing product made with a fiber mat of equivalent weight containing a homogenous mat structure.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A fiber mat comprises: a unitary assembly of fibers comprising at least a first set of fibers and at least a first binder comprising an organic resin, wherein the unitary assembly of fibers comprises a minority portion and a majority portion different than the minority portion, wherein the fiber mat provides at least a 5% increase in tear when placed in a bituminous roofing product compared to an equivalent bituminous roofing product made with a fiber mat of equivalent weight containing a homogenous mat structure. 2. The fiber mat of claim 1, wherein the minority portion has a higher tensile strength than a tensile strength of the majority portion. 3. The fiber mat of claim 1, wherein the majority portion, when placed in a bituminous roofing product, provides the increase in tear when compared to the minority portion. 4. The fiber mat of claim 1, wherein the minority portion is a plane parallel to the plane of the assembly of fibers portion of the unitary assembly of fibers. 5. The fiber mat of claim 4, wherein the minority portion is closer in distance to a top surface of the fiber mat compared to a bottom surface of the fiber mat. 6. The fiber mat of claim 5, wherein the fiber mat has an asymmetric flexibility. 7. The fiber mat of claim 4, wherein the minority portion is approximately equidistant from a top surface of the fiber mat and a bottom surface of the fiber mat. 8. The fiber mat of claim 1, wherein the minority portion comprises a higher concentration of the first binder than the amount of a binder on the majority portion. 9. The fiber mat of claim 8, wherein a ratio of a coefficient of friction of a bottom- bottom to a top-top of the fiber mat is greater than 1.25. 10. The fiber mat of claim 1, wherein the organic resin comprises a urea- formaldehyde composition, a latex composition, a styrene-butadiene rubber (SBR) composition, a vinyl acetate ethylene composition, a blend or combination thereof. 11. The fiber mat of claim 1, wherein the assembly of fibers is a non- woven mat. 12. The fiber mat of claim 1, wherein the fiber mat has a caliper at 1.8 pounds per 100 square feet of about 10 mil to about 40 mil. 13. The fiber mat of claim 1, wherein the fiber mat is used as a reinforcement for a construction product. 14. A method of making a fiber mat comprises: providing a unitary assembly of fibers comprising at least a first set of fibers; 

 applying at least a first binder to the unitary assembly of fibers, wherein the at least first binder comprises an organic resin; and curing the at least first binder, wherein the unitary assembly of fibers comprises a minority portion and a majority portion different than the minority portion, wherein the fiber mat provides at least a 5% increase in tear when placed in a bituminous roofing product compared to an equivalent bituminous roofing product made with a fiber mat of equivalent weight containing a homogenous mat structure. 15. A bituminous roofing product comprises a fiber mat comprising a unitary assembly of fibers comprising at least a first set of fibers; and at least a first binder comprising an organic resin, wherein the unitary assembly of fibers comprises a minority portion and a majority portion different than the minority portion, wherein the fiber mat provides at least a 5% increase in tear when placed in a bituminous roofing product compared to an equivalent bituminous roofing product when made with a fiber mat of equivalent weight containing a homogenous mat structure; and at least one coating of asphalt.
